perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Receiving Party is required to disclose any portion of any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party (i) by operation of law or in connection with a judicial or governmental proceeding or arbitration (whether by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process), (ii) pursuant to the rules or regulations of the United States Securities and Exchange Commission (or any other applicable securities regulatory body) or (iii) pursuant to the rules or regulations of any securities exchange on which the Receiving Party’s or its parent company’s securities are listed or similar self- regulatory body, then such disclosure will be permissible only if and after the Receiving Party promptly notifies the Disclosing Party of such requirement (to the extent legally permitted) so that the Disclosing Party (at its sole cost and expense) may seek an appropriate protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. In no event will the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives oppose any action by the Disclosing Party to obtain an appropriate protective order or assurance of confidential treatment to preserve the confidentiality of any such Confidential Information. If as a result of any such requirement the Receiving Party is required to disclose Confidential Information or the existence, content or status of negotiations relating to the specific transactions contemplated by the Potential Transaction, the Receiving Party may furnish, without liability hereunder, that portion (and only that portion) of the Confidential Information which it is required to disclose.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Receiving Party is required to disclose any portion of any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party (i) by operation of law or in connection with a judicial or governmental proceeding or arbitration (whether by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process), (ii) pursuant to the rules or regulations of the United States Securities and Exchange Commission (or any other applicable securities regulatory body) or (iii) pursuant to the rules or regulations of any securities exchange on which the Receiving Party’s or its parent company’s securities are listed or similar self- regulatory body, then such disclosure will be permissible only if the Receiving Party deems it necessary and notifies the Disclosing Party as soon as reasonably possible. In no event will the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives oppose any action by the Disclosing Party to obtain an appropriate protective order or assurance of confidential treatment to preserve the confidentiality of any such Confidential Information. If as a result of any such requirement the Receiving Party is required to disclose Confidential Information or the existence, content or status of negotiations relating to the specific transactions contemplated by the Potential Transaction, the Receiving Party may furnish, without liability hereunder, that portion (and only that portion) of the Confidential Information which it is required to disclose.
perturbation.explanation: The original text sets out a clear process where disclosure is allowed only 'if and after' prompt notification to the Disclosing Party, allowing them to seek protective measures. The modified version introduces an element of discretion, stating disclosure is permissible 'only if the Receiving Party deems it necessary', without clarifying the criteria for 'necessary' and also replacing the time restriction from 'promptly notifies' to 'as soon as reasonably possible', which creates an in-text contradiction because it allows for the Receiving Party to determine what is deemed necessary, creating grounds for potential disputes. This will affect the Disclosing party if this change is in place, due to them having the sole cost and expense to seek and appropriate protective order. If the Receiving Party deems it is not necessary, then the Disclosing Party could have its confidential information being disclosed and no protective order is granted. This will create arguments on how the information should be protected and when should the Disclosing Party be advised.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclosure Compelled by Law or Judicial Proceeding. In the event that the Receiving Party is required to disclose any portion of any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party (i) by operation of law or in connection with a judicial or governmental proceeding or arbitration (whether by oral questions, interrogatories, requests for information, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process), (ii) pursuant to the rules or regulations of the United States Securities and Exchange Commission (or any other applicable securities regulatory body) or (iii) pursuant to the rules or regulations of any securities exchange on which the Receiving Party’s or its parent company’s securities are listed or similar self- regulatory body, then such disclosure will be permissible only if the Receiving Party deems it necessary and notifies the Disclosing Party as soon as reasonably possible. In no event will the Receiving Party or any of its Representatives oppose any action by the Disclosing Party to obtain an appropriate protective order or assurance of confidential treatment to preserve the confidentiality of any such Confidential Information. If as a result of any such requirement the Receiving Party is required to disclose Confidential Information or the existence, content or status of negotiations relating to the specific transactions contemplated by the Potential Transaction, the Receiving Party may furnish, without liability hereunder, that portion (and only that portion) of the Confidential Information which it is required to disclose.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates disclosure is permissible 'if and after' notification, giving the Disclosing Party a chance to protect its information. The changed text adds the condition that disclosure is permissible 'only if the Receiving Party deems it necessary' and changes the time constraint. This introduces an element of discretion ('deems it necessary') and delays the time ('as soon as reasonably possible'), which directly contradicts the purpose of the original clause by giving the Receiving Party more power. The changed text and contradicted text are the same, and the changed text contradicts the meaning of what was previously said because it is now possible for the Receiving party to withhold confidential information. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' type.

perturbation.original_text: Each party understands that either party may have, or in the future may enter into, relationships with third parties having pre-existing relationships with the other party. Provided that each party complies with its obligations contained herein, and except as otherwise expressly provided herein, this Agreement shall not in any way limit, restrict or preclude either party from pursuing any of its present or future business activities or interests or from entering into any agreement or transaction with any person, regardless of whether such business activities or interests are competitive with the business activities and interests of the other party and regardless of whether the subject matter of any such agreement or transaction is in any way similar to or different from the transactions considered and evaluated by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party understands that either party may have, or in the future may enter into, relationships with third parties having pre-existing relationships with the other party. Provided that each party complies with most of its obligations contained herein, and except as otherwise expressly provided herein, this Agreement shall not in any way limit, restrict or preclude either party from pursuing any of its present or future business activities or interests or from entering into any agreement or transaction with any person, regardless of whether such business activities or interests are competitive with the business activities and interests of the other party and regardless of whether the subject matter of any such agreement or transaction is in any way similar to or different from the transactions considered and evaluated by the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires that each party comply with 'its obligations' for the section to be applicable. The modification changes 'its obligations' to 'most of its obligations,' creating ambiguity. This introduces an in-text contradiction to the contract because it is unclear what constitutes 'most,' leading to disputes over compliance. This will raise arguments for the parties, because each one will be making arguments on what 'most of the obligations are, and when they are in compliance or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Competitive Activities . Each party understands that either party may have, or in the future may enter into, relationships with third parties having pre-existing relationships with the other party. Provided that each party complies with most of its obligations contained herein, and except as otherwise expressly provided herein, this Agreement shall not in any way limit, restrict or preclude either party from pursuing any of its present or future business activities or interests or from entering into any agreement or transaction with any person, regardless of whether such business activities or interests are competitive with the business activities and interests of the other party and regardless of whether the subject matter of any such agreement or transaction is in any way similar to or different from the transactions considered and evaluated by the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each party must comply with 'its obligations' for the agreement to be in effect. The changed text modifies this to 'most of its obligations'. This creates ambiguity because it's unclear what constitutes 'most', leading to potential disputes over whether a party is in compliance. The contradicted text contains the same 'most of its obligations' language, so the contradiction already exists due to the ambiguous change from the original 'its obligations' phrase to the 'most of its obligations' phrase.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the full and complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, representations and understandings, whether oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the full and complete understanding of the parties with respect to most of the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, representations and understandings, whether oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause is an integration clause stating the agreement represents the 'full and complete understanding'. The modified version changes this to 'most of the subject matter hereof,'. This creates an in-text contradiction by casting doubt on whether the written agreement truly encapsulates the entire agreement between the parties. It creates ambiguity regarding which parts are covered and which are not, and opens the door to disputes based on alleged oral agreements. This section of the agreements states the complete understanding, but the in-text perturbation now means, that this is not the complete understanding for most of the subject matter of the agreement. This means that this section of the document directly contradicts the changed text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General Provisions. (a) The parties shall maintain reasonable security measures to safeguard each other’s personally identifiable information from loss, misuse, unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration or destruction. (b) The parties shall supply personally identifiable information to each other only in accordance with, and to the extent permitted by, applicable laws relating to privacy and data protection in the applicable territories. Personally identifiable information supplied by Company to Sony Pictures will be retained and used in accordance with the Sony Pictures Safe Harbor Privacy Policy, located at http://www.sonypictures.com/corp/eu_safe_harbor.html . (c) This Agreement contains the full and complete understanding of the parties with respect to most of the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, representations and understandings, whether oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof. (d) This Agreement may not be amended, waived or modified except by a single instrument in writing executed by duly authorized representatives of the parties. (e) This Agreement will be fairly interpreted and construed in accordance with its terms and without strict interpretation or construction in favor of or against either party. (f) For purposes of this Agreement, the term “person” shall be broadly interpreted to include without limitation any corporation, company, group, partnership and natural person. (g) In the event that any provision of this Agreement is held by a court of competent jurisdiction or arbitrator to be void, voidable, unlawful or for any reason unenforceable, in whole or in part, such provision will be deemed and construed to extend only to the maximum permitted by law so as to effectuate the intent of the parties, and the remainder of this Agreement will continue in full force and effect and enforceable according to its terms. (h) To the extent that any Confidential Information may include materials subject to the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or any other applicable privilege concerning pending or threatened legal proceedings or governmental investigations, each party hereto understands and agrees that both parties hereto and their Representatives have a commonality of interest with respect to such matters and it is the desire, intention and mutual understanding of both parties hereto that the sharing of such Confidential Information is not intended to, and shall not, waive or diminish in any way the confidentiality of such Confidential Information or its continued protection under the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or other applicable privilege. All Confidential Information provided by either party hereto that is entitled to protection under the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or other applicable privilege shall remain entitled to such protection under those privileges, this Agreement, and under the joint defense doctrine. CONFIDENTIAL 7(i) This Agreement may be executed by manual or facsimile or other electronically transmitted signatures and in any number of counterparts, each of which will be deemed an original and all which together will constitute one and the same instrument. [SIGNATURE PAGE FOLLOWS]
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the integration clause, stating that the agreement contains the 'full and complete understanding of the parties with respect to most of the subject matter hereof.' This directly contradicts the text in section (c) of the General Provisions, which states: 'This Agreement contains the full and complete understanding of the parties with respect to most of the subject matter hereof.' While both sections are identically worded after the change, the change itself creates the contradiction as the purpose of the integration clause is to assert complete understanding of all subject matter, now it admits that not all subject matter is fully understood. This introduces ambiguity and inconsistency, making the agreement internally contradictory.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended, waived or modified except by a single instrument in writing executed by duly authorized representatives of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended, waived or modified by a simple verbal conversation, as long as both parties agree with each other. A written instrument is not necessary, and it is not mandatory that they are executed by the duly authorized representatives of the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The initial statement that 'This Agreement may not be amended, waived or modified except by a single instrument in writing executed by duly authorized representatives of the parties' is directly negated by changing the text, allowing a simple verbal conversation, creating an in-text contradiction, because it is directly contradicting the first statment of the section. This contradiction means that it creates an ambiguity of the real process of modifying, waiving, or amending the agreement. This will create problems with the contract, because no agreements will ever be official, and it will be based on each parties word, which would be untraceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General Provisions. (a) The parties shall maintain reasonable security measures to safeguard each other’s personally identifiable information from loss, misuse, unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration or destruction. (b) The parties shall supply personally identifiable information to each other only in accordance with, and to the extent permitted by, applicable laws relating to privacy and data protection in the applicable territories. Personally identifiable information supplied by Company to Sony Pictures will be retained and used in accordance with the Sony Pictures Safe Harbor Privacy Policy, located at http://www.sonypictures.com/corp/eu_safe_harbor.html . (c) This Agreement contains the full and complete understanding of the parties with respect to most of the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, representations and understandings, whether oral or written, with respect to the subject matter hereof. (d) This Agreement may be amended, waived or modified by a simple verbal conversation, as long as both parties agree with each other. A written instrument is not necessary, and it is not mandatory that they are executed by the duly authorized representatives of the parties. (e) This Agreement will be fairly interpreted and construed in accordance with its terms and without strict interpretation or construction in favor of or against either party. (f) For purposes of this Agreement, the term “person” shall be broadly interpreted to include without limitation any corporation, company, group, partnership and natural person. (g) In the event that any provision of this Agreement is held by a court of competent jurisdiction or arbitrator to be void, voidable, unlawful or for any reason unenforceable, in whole or in part, such provision will be deemed and construed to extend only to the maximum permitted by law so as to effectuate the intent of the parties, and the remainder of this Agreement will continue in full force and effect and enforceable according to its terms. (h) To the extent that any Confidential Information may include materials subject to the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or any other applicable privilege concerning pending or threatened legal proceedings or governmental investigations, each party hereto understands and agrees that both parties hereto and their Representatives have a commonality of interest with respect to such matters and it is the desire, intention and mutual understanding of both parties hereto that the sharing of such Confidential Information is not intended to, and shall not, waive or diminish in any way the confidentiality of such Confidential Information or its continued protection under the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or other applicable privilege. All Confidential Information provided by either party hereto that is entitled to protection under the attorney-client privilege, work product doctrine or other applicable privilege shall remain entitled to such protection under those privileges, this Agreement, and under the joint defense doctrine. CONFIDENTIAL 7(i) This Agreement may be executed by manual or facsimile or other electronically transmitted signatures and in any number of counterparts, each of which will be deemed an original and all which together will constitute one and the same instrument. [SIGNATURE PAGE FOLLOWS]
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly contradicts both the original text and the contradicted text. The original text states that the agreement can only be amended, waived, or modified by a written instrument executed by authorized representatives. The changed text allows for verbal modification without written documentation or authorized representatives. The contradicted text contains the changed text, therefore, contradicting itself, as it also contains the initial statement that was written in the Original Text, creating an in-text contradiction.
